title: feat: add p0 reaction dispatch safe candidate persistence
goal: Add local/internal safe candidate persistence for reaction dispatch using Support Event Contract v2 validation, without dispatching runtime side effects or exposing raw data.
allowedScope: Support Event Contract v2 validator alignment
list and detail admin candidate views
doneCriteria: candidate endpoints require admin bearer token
candidate creation uses Support Event Contract v2 validation
candidate creation is idempotent by safe context and constraints hashes
list and detail return safe metadata only
no reaction, overlay, or outbox enqueue
no raw message, raw payload, wallet address, authorization, secrets, prompts, LLM output, or private URLs
securityBoundaries: No DB driver, Redis, or Kafka dependency is added.
No real DB connection is introduced.
No migration is changed or executed.
No real YouTube API or OAuth token is used.
No real TTS, Live2D, renderer, OBS, WebSocket delivery, IRIS Core call, or VOXWEAVE call is performed.
No reaction, overlay, or outbox side effects are enqueued.
No RPC, wallet, deploy, contracts, workflows, web, or overlay app changes.

Candidate metadata excludes raw messages, raw payloads, wallet addresses, authorization strings, secrets, stdout, stderr, jobs_url, logs_url, full prompts, LLM output, model-generated TTS text, and private URLs.
No runtime, production, legal, or YouTube policy readiness is claimed.
residualRisks: This is local/internal reaction dispatch candidate persistence only.
Actual admin approval, durable production persistence, and external execution adapters remain future scoped work.
humanConfirmation: AI review is not human approval.
AI review is not GitHub approval review.
This PR does not create owner approval record.
This PR does not create merge authority.
productVerificationCommands: cd contracts && forge test || true: unavailable_nonblocking

packageVerification.verification: No package scripts, runtime dependencies, DB driver dependency, Redis/Kafka dependency, package.json, or pnpm-lock changes are introduced.

apiCompatibilitySummary.statement: Adds local/internal admin reaction dispatch safe candidate metadata endpoints.

runtimeSmokeRationale: No runtime readiness is claimed; this is local/internal candidate metadata only.
rollbackOrStopCondition: Stop if package.json, pnpm-lock, contracts, migrations, workflows, apps/web, apps/overlay, real YouTube API, real OAuth, real RPC, real DB, DB driver dependency, Redis/Kafka dependency, wallet/RPC/deploy changes, real TTS/Live2D/renderer/OBS/WebSocket delivery, IRIS Core/VOXWEAVE calls, support event mutation, reaction/overlay/outbox enqueue, or readiness/compliance claims are introduced.

reviewScope.scope: P0 reaction dispatch safe candidate persistence, admin auth, Support Event Contract v2 alignment, safe metadata, idempotency, list/detail views, tests, docs, and .codex evidence.
reviewScope.riskSummary: Main risk is raw data exposure, support event mutation, dispatch side effects, external runtime execution, or readiness claims; tests and docs reject those outcomes.
reviewScope.verificationOracle: Candidate persistence tests, contract v2 admin surface tests, validator tests, reaction dispatch preview tests, full repository tests, evidence checks, quality self-protection, secret scan, and GitHub same-head checks.
